violations.text: Food debris was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

Working containers of spices (tray on shelf) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff placed labels during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025
A container of heavy cream was observed in direct contact with food in the prep unit. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Curry sauce debris was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
The handwashing sink in the kitchen and bathroom are lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Garlic butter was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit double-stacked above the fill line in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC chose to dispose. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

Chicken was observed thawing in a container of stagnant water on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. PIC placed under running water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the small reach-in and table-top display refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Working containers of dry spices and sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025
Containers of heavy cream were observed in direct contact with garlic and herbs in the prep unit. PIC disposed affected product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients and prepared foods in refrigeration. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils were brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Blender bowl was observed with pooled water. PIC brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
The shared outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
The shared grease receptacle has an accumulation of grease residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 200 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC diluted concentration during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Staff began labeling during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff began labeling during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025
The grease receptacle has an accumulation of grease debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable slicer, knives, blender, hotel pans) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2025

Chicken and rice was observed held at 90-128 degrees Fahrenheit in the steam table and hot box. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2025

Teas and yogurts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025
